Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: The agent searches Linear for all tickets belonging to the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues (with the project filter, or after discovering the project via linear_list_projects). It finds ~16 tickets ranging from ENG-201 to ENG-220. Critically, it notices that many tickets have stale statuses for example ENG-205 through ENG-209 still show "todo" and ENG-214, 215, 216 still show "in_progress" despite the underlying work being completed weeks ago. ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, assigned to Lena) and ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, assigned to Samira) are the two tickets the agent needs to pay special attention to.
OE 2: The agent reads comments on key Linear tickets using linear_list_comments, particularly on ENG-211 (pilot monitoring), ENG-212 (edge case tests), ENG-216 (audit), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening), and ENG-220 (duplicate detection). It finds Dmitri's audit results (47 expenses reviewed, 5 false rejections for Vectral, 3 false approvals for Mosaic, $885 total exposure), Dmitri's 6 schema validation tests, Lena's statement that she will incorporate those tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate, and Samira's remediation plan listing all the dependent tickets and timelines.
OE 3: The agent searches Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with queries related to the expense auto-categorizer, remediation, pipeline integration, or similar terms. It finds the engineering team's extensive discussion of the pilot failure Samira's remediation plan, Dmitri's full audit results, Lena's confirmation about integrating validation tests into ENG-217, Emeka's discovery of Mosaic false approvals, Mina's report of Vectral false rejections, and Marcus's announcement that he placed financial holds on two duplicate receipt bills (STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097). The agent should find NO deployment confirmation for ENG-217 Lena acknowledged the plan but never posted that the pipeline hardening was actually shipped.
OE 4: The agent searches emails using search_emails with queries like "expense auto-categorizer" or "pilot audit" or "remediation." It finds Elena's directive to suspend auto-approve/deny, Samira's remediation plan and audit summary to Elena (47 expenses, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 exposure), Samira's detailed audit spreadsheet email to Marcus ($400 + $350 + $135 = $885), Mina's false rejection reports (Danielle Osei $475, Kevin Tran $185), Emeka's false approval reports (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, $350 coworking), and Marcus's email to Dmitri confirming the QuickBooks status of all three duplicate receipts (Kevin Tran's $89 already paid out on 3/8, Andre Washington's $89 and Jordan Ekwueme's $45 held).
OE 5: The agent checks Airtable stipend records for the Vectral false rejections using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the "Stipend Transactions" table (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001). It discovers that 3 of the 5 false rejections were never corrected: Maria Santos ($550 standing desk, recStip000000018), James Okafor ($82 keyboard tray, recStip000000019), and Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi, recStip000000020) all still show "Rejected." The other 2 Danielle Osei ($475 chair) and Kevin Tran ($185 internet) were already manually approved by Mina.
OE 6: The agent checks Airtable stipend records for the Mosaic false approvals. It finds that all 3 incorrect approvals still show "Approved": Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (exceeds $800 limit by $400), Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership (coworking excluded from Mosaic policy entirely), and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes (same exclusion bug). The total false approval exposure is $885, which MoveOps is absorbing per Elena's directive.
OE 7: The agent checks Airtable for the three flagged duplicate receipt submissions using airtable_search_records. It finds: Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, "Approved" already paid out on 3/8), Andre Washington's $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, "Pending Approval" held), and Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, "Pending Approval" held). Two are on hold, one was disbursed before the audit caught it.
OE 8: The agent checks QuickBooks for the actual financial holds using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "stipend" or the doc numbers STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097. It finds two bills with ADMIN HOLDs placed on 2026-03-15 by Marcus Thorne: one for Andre Washington ($89, duplicate receipt hash flagged) and one for Jordan Ekwueme ($45, PDF template metadata match flagged). Both holds are still active payments have not been released.
OE 9: The agent updates the stale Linear ticket statuses using linear_update_issue to change them to "done" where Slack, email, and comment evidence confirms the work was completed. This includes ENG-205 (CategoryMatcher), ENG-206 (PolicyValidator), ENG-207 (DecisionEngine), ENG-208 (Rule Config UI), ENG-209 (Policy Ingestion) from todo to done, and ENG-214 (Vectral config fix), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix), ENG-216 (full audit) from in_progress to done. The agent also adds comments via linear_create_comment on each updated ticket explaining the status correction.
OE 10: The agent corrects the 3 still-rejected Vectral stipend records using airtable_update_records on table tblStipends00001 (base appMoveOpsOps001). It updates recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, $550), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, $82), and recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, $130) changing their Approval Status from "Rejected" to "Approved" and setting the correct Approved Amount. These records have been stuck in the wrong state since March despite the audit determining they should have been approved.
OE 11: The agent updates the launch readiness ticket ENG-219 (issue_launch_readiness_checklist) using linear_update_issue and/or linear_create_comment. The update includes what is confirmed done (ENG-205–209, 214, 215, 216), what is still blocking (ENG-217 pipeline hardening has no evidence of deployment, ENG-212 edge cases still in progress, ENG-218 dashboard validation still in progress, ENG-220 duplicate detection not started), the stipend correction status, the financial hold status, and an overall NO-GO assessment the relaunch cannot proceed until at minimum ENG-217 is confirmed deployed.
OE 12: The agent posts a remediation status summary in the #engineering Slack channel (channel_id: C003) using conversations_add_message. The post covers: which ticket statuses were corrected, the 3 Vectral stipend records that were fixed, the duplicate receipt hold status (2 active, 1 already paid), the pipeline integration status (ENG-217 unverified the critical blocker), remaining items before relaunch, total financial exposure, and the NO-GO verdict.
OE 13: The agent looks up the email addresses for Samira Tariq and Lena Björkström using contacts_search_contacts with queries like "Samira" and "Lena." It finds samira.tariq@moveops.com and lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com.
OE 14: The agent sends the readiness assessment email to Samira using send_email with sender dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, recipient samira.tariq@moveops.com, and CC lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com. The email contains the full go/no-go assessment: pass/fail status for each launch gate, total financial exposure (approximately $1,870 combining the $885 in false approvals absorbed by MoveOps, $762 in uncorrected false rejections owed to Vectral employees, and $223 in duplicate receipt exposure), the critical blocker (ENG-217 pipeline hardening no evidence of deployment), and recommendations for the path to relaunch. Lena is CC'd because the pipeline hardening is her responsibility.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: The agent searches Linear for all tickets belonging to the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues (with the project filter, or after discovering the project via linear_list_projects). ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, assigned to Lena) and ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, assigned to Samira) are the two tickets the agent needs to pay special attention to.
OE 2: The agent reads comments on key Linear tickets using linear_list_comments, particularly on ENG-211 (pilot monitoring), ENG-212 (edge case tests), ENG-216 (audit), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening), and ENG-220 (duplicate detection). It finds Dmitri's audit results (47 expenses reviewed, 5 false rejections for Vectral, 3 false approvals for Mosaic, $885 total exposure), Dmitri's 6 schema validation tests, Lena's statement that she will incorporate those tests into the ENG-217 deployment gate, and Samira's remediation plan listing all the dependent tickets and timelines.

The agent should find NO deployment confirmation for ENG-217 Lena acknowledged the plan but never posted that the pipeline hardening was actually shipped.
OE 4: The agent searches emails using search_emails with queries like "expense auto-categorizer" or "pilot audit" or "remediation." OE 6: The agent checks Airtable stipend records for the Mosaic false approvals. It finds that all 3 incorrect approvals still show "Approved": Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (exceeds $800 limit by $400), Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership (coworking excluded from Mosaic policy entirely), and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes (same exclusion bug). The total false approval exposure is $885, which MoveOps is absorbing per Elena's directive.
OE 7: The agent checks Airtable for the three flagged duplicate receipt submissions using airtable_search_records. It finds: Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, "Approved" already paid out on 3/8), Andre Washington's $89 keyboard (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, "Pending Approval" held), and Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, "Pending Approval" held). OE 8: The agent checks QuickBooks for the actual financial holds using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "stipend" or the doc numbers STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097. It finds two bills with ADMIN HOLDs placed on 2026-03-15 by Marcus Thorne: one for Andre Washington ($89, duplicate receipt hash flagged) and one for Jordan Ekwueme ($45, PDF template metadata match flagged). Both holds are still active payments have not been released.
OE 9: The agent updates the stale Linear ticket statuses using linear_update_issue to change them to "done" where Slack, email, and comment evidence confirms the work was completed. This includes ENG-205 (CategoryMatcher), ENG-206 (PolicyValidator), ENG-207 (DecisionEngine), ENG-208 (Rule Config UI), ENG-209 (Policy Ingestion) from todo to done, and ENG-214 (Vectral config fix), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix), ENG-216 (full audit) from in_progress to done. The agent also adds comments via linear_create_comment on each updated ticket explaining the status correction.
OE 10: The agent corrects the 3 still-rejected Vectral stipend records using airtable_update_records on table tblStipends00001 (base appMoveOpsOps001). It updates recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, $550), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, $82), and recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, $130) changing their Approval Status from "Rejected" to "Approved" and setting the correct Approved Amount. These records have been stuck in the wrong state since March despite the audit determining they should have been approved.
OE 11: The agent updates the launch readiness ticket ENG-219 (issue_launch_readiness_checklist) using linear_update_issue and/or linear_create_comment. The update includes what is confirmed done (ENG-205–209, 214, 215, 216), what is still blocking (ENG-217 pipeline hardening has no evidence of deployment, ENG-212 edge cases still in progress, ENG-218 dashboard validation still in progress, ENG-220 duplicate detection not started), the stipend correction status, the financial hold status, and an overall NO-GO assessment the relaunch cannot proceed until at minimum ENG-217 is confirmed deployed.
OE 12: The agent posts a remediation status summary in the #engineering Slack channel (channel_id: C003) using conversations_add_message. The post covers: which ticket statuses were corrected, the 3 Vectral stipend records that were fixed, the duplicate receipt hold status (2 active, 1 already paid), the pipeline integration status (ENG-217 unverified the critical blocker), remaining items before relaunch, total financial exposure, and the NO-GO verdict.
OE 13: The agent looks up the email addresses for Samira Tariq and Lena Björkström using contacts_search_contacts with queries like "Samira" and "Lena." It finds samira.tariq@moveops.com and lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com.
OE 14: The agent sends the readiness assessment email to Samira using send_email with sender dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, recipient samira.tariq@moveops.com, and CC lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com. The email contains the full go/no-go assessment: pass/fail status for each launch gate, total financial exposure (approximately $1,870 combining the $885 in false approvals absorbed by MoveOps, $762 in uncorrected false rejections owed to Vectral employees, and $223 in duplicate receipt exposure), the critical blocker (ENG-217 pipeline hardening no evidence of deployment), and recommendations for the path to relaunch. Lena is CC'd because the pipeline hardening is her responsibility.
